scope: apps/com/src visible strings plus shared compliance constants

blockingRules.description: No income, earnings, compensation, commission, rank, cycle, CV, bonus, or payout claims.
No placement guarantees, spillover promises, binary-leg promises, or downline projections.
No AI prospecting, automated calling, AI lead qualification, scoring, or ranking language.
No current team head count; the 100,000 goal is allowed.
No THREE International company branding, logo references, or promoter disclaimers.
No programmatic enrollment, registration, or company handoff route language.

allowedSignalRules.description: GLP-THREE product naming is allowed when it does not name THREE International.
Public market figures and product-category cost context are allowed when not tied to earnings.
The 100,000 team goal is allowed; current team head count is not.
Prospect-facing PMV language is People, Momentum, Volume, and Checks.
Queue, placement, and beneath-you language is allowed only as team activity demonstration.
The canonical .com disclaimer is allowed only through packages/shared/src/compliance.ts.

You were not chosen randomly. You were chosen deliberately.
What you are about to see is three things happening at the same time. A category-defining product. A team that is forming in this exact window. A moment in the market that won't come around again.
That is what the invitation is about. Not a sales call - a briefing on all three. Read it the way you would read a memo from a friend who has done the homework.

The team you are about to see is being built right now, in real time. This is the mechanism we are so excited about - how our teams are actually built, and why the opportunity in front of you is real. We wanted you to see it for yourself.
See the team forming around you →
A Team Magnificent Message · Team Magnificent
You may have heard a brief message first. This page gives you the fuller context: the product, the timing, and why it was worth sending.
No pressure, no obligation - just clear information to consider.
A short message can only point to the door. The page in front of you gives the full picture: the product, the timing, and the team activity behind this moment.
Start with the video, then use the rest of the page as a reference. It is information you can consider privately and talk through if it makes sense.

GLP-THREE launched January 2026 · trademark and patent pending
$6.8T global wellness economy · the category is moving
72% of American adults are overweight · the market is wide open
Real people. Real results. No injections, no prescription.
Take your time with what follows.
Watch Dr. Dan. Then Everything Makes Sense.
Dr. Dan Gubler · Chief Scientific Officer & Chief Formulator
Dr. Dan Gubler GLP-THREE product video
Dr. Dan Gubler — GLP-THREE product video
Open Dr. Dan's GLP-THREE video in YouTube, then come back and continue.
When the video finishes, continue to your live team position.
Then scroll. The rest of this page expands on what he just told you.
average synthetic GLP-1 cost without insurance
And A Problem Nobody Has Solved.
You are watching a generational shift in how people manage weight. It has already started. It is accelerating.
What Is Already In The Market.
The injectable GLP-1 category is the reason the world is paying attention. It works. It also has a profile worth understanding.

Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro and others belong to a class called GLP-1 receptor agonists. They mimic a hormone the body makes naturally and signal the brain that you are full. Appetite quiets. Stomach emptying slows. People lose weight.
Delivery is by injection — weekly for the most common dosing. Use is typically continuous; the effect tapers when the injections stop.
What it costs to be on it
Out-of-pocket cost runs roughly $1,000 to $1,500 per month. Many insurance plans do not cover weight management on these drugs, leaving the cost on the patient.

The known side-effect profile includes nausea, slowed gastric emptying (gastroparesis), pancreatitis risk, lean muscle loss, and a recognizable change in facial appearance the press has labeled "Ozempic face."
Pattern users describe: get on, results come, go off, weight returns.
None of this is a knock on the category. It is the starting line everyone is comparing against. What comes next on this page is how that comparison changed.
MBC-267 peptide complex supporting your body's own GLP-1 signaling.
Liquid dropper. ¾ of a dropper, 30 minutes before a meal.
Nausea, gastric slowing, pancreatitis risk, muscle loss.
All-natural ingredients. Trademark and patent pending.
Designed to be sustainable — built around what the body already does.
Natural. Patented. No Needle. No Prescription.
GLP-THREE was built for the same outcome the category is chasing — through a different door. Same destination. Different mechanism. Different profile. Different cost.

A patented peptide complex found naturally in salmon and mushrooms. It supports your body's own GLP-1 signaling rather than substituting for it. The peptide is delivered orally as a liquid dropper — ¾ of a dropper, thirty minutes before a meal.
GLP-THREE is built around MBC-267, a patented peptide complex found naturally in salmon and certain mushrooms.
MBC-267 supports the body's own GLP-1 signaling pathway rather than substituting a synthetic agonist. The mechanism is metabolic support, not chemical replacement.
The full ingredient list is on the bottle and in the PDF dossier below.
Trademark and patent pending. The peptide complex itself is patented; the formulation is proprietary.

Dr. Dan Gubler — Chief Scientific Officer and Chief Formulator — developed the product. His scientific record: Caltech PhD in Organic Chemistry, sixteen patents, more than seventy supplements formulated across his career, a top-50 podcast on supplement science, and a public following of 1.3 million.
Manufactured to current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) standards.
Quality, identity, and potency are verified at multiple stages of production. The dossier below contains the manufacturing summary.
GLP-THREE is taken as a liquid dropper.
Three-quarters of a dropper, thirty minutes before a meal.
Take consistently. The mechanism is metabolic — results compound with daily use, not from a single dose.
Is GLP-THREE a drug? No. It is a dietary supplement designed to support your body's own GLP-1 signaling. It is not a prescription drug, not an injection, and not a GLP-1 receptor agonist.
Do I need a prescription? No.
Can I take it with other medications? Talk to your physician before combining any supplement with prescribed medication — including GLP-THREE.
How long does a bottle last? Standard daily use is roughly one bottle per month. The dossier below has the exact specifications.
Five short briefings for the parts of the page that warranted more than a sentence. Open what you want. Skip what you do not.
Kevin Gardner before and after using GLP-THREE — down 19 lbs, no injections, with measurements
Kevin Gardner. Down 19 lbs. No injections.

Two years ago, almost nobody outside the medical world had heard of GLP-1. Today it is on the cover of every magazine and in every other ad break. The category is mainstream. The market is paying attention.
Every major movement in wellness eventually produces a natural alternative to the pharmaceutical version. The injectable category created the demand. The shelf for a natural answer has been empty.
GLP-THREE launched in the third week of January 2026 with trademark and patent pending. The first all-natural GLP-1 replacement on the market. The window is opening, not closing.
Three things are converging at once.
The moment all three are true is now.
Five years from now, everyone will know about this category. The people who moved first will have built something that runs without them. The people who waited will have watched.
